# Preferences Template

```yaml
# Superpowers Workflow Preferences
# Auto-generated by the onboarding wizard.
# Edit manually or ask the agent to update.

workflow:
  auto_commit: <true|false>
  confirm_destructive_actions: <true|false>

communication:
  language: <language-code>

# Platform-specific settings.
# The `copilot:` section is silently ignored by Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI.
# rubber_duck: true forces Rubber Duck at every critical checkpoint.
# rubber_duck: false (default) uses Copilot CLI's native invocation behavior.
# review_final: run the native `review` skill at the final review as an extra bug-focused pass on top
#   of the default final reviewer. Additive (spec + quality gates always run); no effort levels.
copilot:
  rubber_duck: false
  review_final: false

# Platform-specific settings.
# The `claude_code:` section is silently ignored by Copilot CLI, Codex, and Gemini CLI.
# All off by default — the user opts in during onboarding (Claude Code only).
# These native skills are ADDITIVE: the spec review and quality review gates always run.
# simplify: run the native /simplify skill on changed code during execution (per task; applies cleanups).
# code_review_final: run /code-review at the final review as an extra bug-focused pass on top
#   of the default final reviewer.
# code_review_effort: low | medium | high | max (ultra unsupported — cloud, separate tier).
claude_code:
  simplify: false
  code_review_final: false
  code_review_effort: medium

# Context settings. Optional — added during onboarding if the user provides corporate artifacts.
# When has_corporate_artifacts is true, the agent reads .superpowers/corporate-artifacts.yml
# for local paths and public URLs to corporate documents (PRDs, specs, UML diagrams, wikis, etc.)
context:
  has_corporate_artifacts: false

# Token optimization settings.
# caveman: true activates ultra-compressed communication in execution/review phases only.
# caveman_level: intensity level (lite | full | ultra | wenyan-lite | wenyan-full | wenyan-ultra).
# Planning, brainstorming, PRD, and communication with the user always use normal mode.
optimization:
  caveman: false
  caveman_level: full

# Persistent memory settings.
# persistent_memory: true enables cross-session memory recall and persistence via .memory/ SQLite database.
# When false (default), all super.persistent-memory operations (recall and persist) are skipped.
# Disabling does NOT delete existing .memory/ data — it only prevents future reads/writes.
memory:
  persistent_memory: false

# Model tier → concrete model mapping for THIS harness (optional).
# Execution skills choose an abstract tier per task (see super.subagent-driven-development
# § Model Selection); this maps each tier to a real model name. Leave a value empty to let
# the harness pick its own cheap/standard/capable model.
model_tiers:
  cheap:
  standard:
  capable:
```
